text_context: When the reference electrode is connected to the middle electrolyte chamber, the measured impedance mainly reflects the resistance from the cathode to middle chamber, which we defined as R1.

    The reference electrode is a slender, transparent glass tube with a black top cap, tapering slightly towards its submerged end. Its lower portion, appearing filled with a colorless liquid, is inserted into a fitting on the top surface of the middle electrolyte chamber.
